description:
Every chrono use in the codebase is Utc::now(), DateTime<Utc>, RFC3339 serialize/parse, and Duration arithmetic — zero local-time or timezone use across ~18 files. chrono's default feature set pulls iana-time-zone (a tz-database lookup crate) for Local, which balls never touches.

WHY: removes the iana-time-zone dependency chain for no behavior change. Pure footprint reduction; the RFC3339-UTC + Duration surface balls actually uses is preserved.

SCOPE: change Cargo.toml to chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["now", "serde"] } (adjust the minimal feature list so Utc::now(), DateTime<Utc>, to_rfc3339*, parse_from_rfc3339, and chrono::Duration::* all still compile); verify iana-time-zone is gone from cargo tree; full test suite + 100% coverage gate must pass. Full chrono removal (hand-rolled RFC3339) was considered and rejected as not worth the parsing risk — do not do that here.
title: Trim chrono default features (drop iana-time-zone)
